payload.answer: Graph memory systems must explicitly model relationships (not just facts) and include provenance mechanisms to handle updates and ensure reliability.

entity_resolution.reason: No existing entity matches the described graph memory design principles.

memory_cards.statement: Graph memory systems enable agents to preserve relationships between knowledge entities, not just isolated facts.
Provenance tracking is essential for graph memory to maintain update semantics and trustworthiness.

details: Durable memory committed with preserved ambiguity (e.g., 'update semantics' left open-ended).
No superseded facts introduced; all statements are current.
Relationships explicitly modeled to avoid isolated fact storage.
